judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable: both reports hit expectations (a)–(d) with proper citations and both miss (e)'s mis-ordering/restructuring point, though Report 2 at least references the lead-with-impact rule.

report_1.evidence: Per `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md:3-7`: failures began 14:03, the first page didn't fire until 14:50 (47 minutes later)

report_2.evidence: The internal timeline shows failures began at 14:03 but the first alert did not fire until 14:50 (47 minutes later) ... (`larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md`)

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md:9-10` states 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` states 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv:6` shows April 2026 uptime at 99.77%. `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md:3-4` defines the contractual monthly SLA as 99.9%, states any month below that is a breach

report_2.evidence: April 2026 uptime was 99.77% (`larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv`), below the contractual 99.9% monthly SLA (`larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md`), which states any month below 99.9% is a breach owing service credits.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1 says "Never assign blame to an individual" ... states "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change ... This needs to be rewritten

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1 explicitly says "Never assign blame to an individual,"
